---
title: "LRPITT16: Modality contrast — taught expressive (TE) vs taught receptive (TR) vocabulary"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

The generalisation contrasts [LRPITT15](../lrp-rli-itt-015) (expressive: TE vs UE) and
[LRPITT15b](../lrp-rli-itt-115) (receptive: TR vs UR) each compare taught vs not-taught
*within* a modality. Neither asks whether the intervention moved the directly-taught words
more in the **expressive** modality than in the **receptive** modality. LRPITT16 supplies that
missing contrast: the two Block 1 *taught* outcomes — expressive taught target words $TE$ and
receptive taught target words $TR$ — are modelled jointly over the randomised window, and the
headline quantity is the difference in intervention benefit, $\tau[TE] - \tau[TR]$.

A positive contrast means the programme moved the taught words more in production than in
comprehension. Both $\tau$ are causal (randomised-window ITT effects), so the contrast is a
clean comparison of two randomised effects — but the two outcomes are on different item
denominators, so it is read on the logit scale.

## Design

Two-outcome joint Beta-Binomial over the randomised phase-0 window ($G = 1$ intervention,
$G = 0$ wait-list), own baseline + linear age, no cross-baselines. LKJ residual correlation is
**off**, mirroring the convergence-safe fallback of the LRPITT15 companions: with
$n \approx 53$ and two outcomes the residual correlation is weakly identified and the
conservative choice widens the difference slightly rather than risk poor mixing.

{{< include _partials/_results_joint.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
